problem: Form submissions can trigger automation rules with conditional branching, but concurrent submission processing must not cause duplicate rule execution
solution: Use SETNX (SET if Not eXists) atomicity to acquire rule execution locks. Each rule per submission has a unique lock key: rule-exec-lock:{submissionId}:{ruleId}. Evaluate rule conditions (field, operator, value) to determine which actions to execute (then-actions vs else-actions). Store rule execution record before releasing lock.

pattern.step_1: Fetch automation rules for the form
pattern.step_3: If lock held: skip rule (idempotency — rule already executed)
pattern.step_4: If lock acquired: evaluate rule.condition against submission data
pattern.step_5: Condition true: queue thenActions; false: queue elseActions
pattern.step_6: Store rule execution record with condition result + actions executed
pattern.step_7: Release lock (DELETE or let TTL expire)

ironRules: IR-1: SETNX(rule-exec-lock) at ORDER 1 for idempotency
IR-2: Rule condition evaluation with multi-path dispatch
IR-3: Queue rule actions (emit, webhook, transform, store)
IR-4: Store execution before releasing lock (DNA-8)
IR-5: tenantId from ALS; rule.tenantId must match
